edges.description: FUCA1 pathogenic variants reduce measured alpha-L-fucosidase enzyme activity.
Enzyme deficiency impairs degradation of fucosylated glycoconjugates.
Storage distends lysosomes and produces secondary lipid storage in the CNS.
edges.target: Neural lysosomal expansion and secondary storage

edges.description: Storage in liver, skin, and other tissues contributes to visceral and dermatologic manifestations.
Accumulated fucosylated glycopeptides and oligosaccharides are excessively excreted in urine.
Altered handling of fucose-containing airway glycoconjugates is suspected to impair mucus clearance.
intermediate_mechanisms: Altered terminal sugar cleavage affecting mucus cross-linking and viscoelasticity.

edges.description: Lysosomal storage disease is associated with dysostosis multiplex and vertebral skeletal abnormalities through incompletely defined intermediates.
Fucosidosis can include conjunctival, retinal, and corneal storage-related ocular involvement.
intermediate_mechanisms: Storage material in conjunctival and retinal vessels with corneal involvement.

edges.description: The multisystem developmental presentation of fucosidosis includes brachycephaly through incompletely defined intermediates.
The multisystem lysosomal storage phenotype includes coarse facial features through incompletely defined craniofacial intermediates.
The multisystem developmental presentation of fucosidosis includes facial dysmorphism through incompletely defined intermediates.
The multisystem craniofacial presentation of fucosidosis includes prominent forehead through incompletely defined intermediates.
The severe multisystem storage phenotype is associated with growth failure through incompletely defined intermediates.
Abnormal urinary storage products are associated with a mucopolysacchariduria-like biochemical phenotype.
The multisystem storage phenotype includes lipoatrophy through incompletely defined intermediates.
The multisystem storage phenotype can include cardiovascular abnormalities through incompletely defined intermediates.
The multisystem storage phenotype can include gallbladder abnormalities through incompletely defined intermediates.
The multisystem storage phenotype includes hypothyroidism through incompletely defined endocrine intermediates.
The multisystem developmental phenotype includes dental abnormalities through incompletely defined intermediates.
The multisystem storage phenotype can include cardiomegaly through incompletely defined cardiovascular intermediates.
Expanded lysosomal storage is associated with inflammatory gliosis and neuronal loss.
edges.source: Neural lysosomal expansion and secondary storage

edges.description: CNS storage disease is associated with oligodendrocyte loss and hypomyelination.
intermediate_mechanisms: Oligodendrocyte loss in CNS storage pathology.
edges.source: Neural lysosomal expansion and secondary storage

edges.description: Neuronal injury and neuroinflammation contribute to severe cognitive impairment.
intermediate_mechanisms: Progressive mental deterioration and neuronal loss.

edges.description: CNS storage pathology and neurodegeneration are associated with developmental delay and psychomotor decline.
Neural injury and inflammation contribute to epilepsy susceptibility.
CNS injury contributes to progressive pyramidal and motor dysfunction.
intermediate_mechanisms: Progressive neurologic degeneration with pyramidal motor involvement.

edges.description: CNS storage pathology and motor deterioration are associated with hypotonia through incompletely defined intermediates.
Progressive neurologic and multisystem disease is associated with decreased muscle mass through incompletely defined intermediates.
White-matter disease contributes to progressive motor impairment.
CNS white-matter involvement can coincide with auditory pathway abnormalities and hearing impairment, but the causal intermediate is incompletely defined.
White-matter and corticospinal motor involvement can produce pyramidal signs through incompletely defined intermediates.
Visceral storage contributes to liver enlargement.
Cutaneous involvement contributes to abnormal sweating.
Cutaneous storage disease is associated with generalized hyperkeratosis through incompletely defined intermediates.
Skin involvement includes telangiectasia and angiokeratoma.
intermediate_mechanisms: Telangiectasia and angiokeratoma in skin vessels.

edges.description: Cutaneous vascular involvement can include acrocyanosis through incompletely defined intermediates.
Cutaneous involvement can include distal nail changes through incompletely defined intermediates.
Impaired local mucus clearance is suspected to increase recurrent respiratory infections.
intermediate_mechanisms: Abnormal mucus viscoelasticity and ineffective ciliary clearance.

edges.description: Skeletal involvement manifests as dysostosis multiplex.
Vertebral skeletal involvement includes anterior tonguing or beaking.
Skeletal involvement can include kyphotic/gibbous spinal deformity.
Ocular storage involvement can include corneal opacity.
Reduced alpha-L-fucosidase activity directly reports the primary FUCA1 lysosomal hydrolase deficiency.
Increased urinary fucose-rich oligosaccharides and glycopeptides report the fucosylated glycoconjugate storage burden caused by alpha-L-fucosidase deficiency.
